name: Product Box Spec Panel
description: Back-of-box product explanation panel with contents, specs, compatibility, proof, shipping notes, and campaign caveats.

box-back.description: Back-of-package grid with feature callouts, contents list, compatibility, shipping status, and support notes.

slots.feature-burst: Two to four high-impact product benefits in bold manual panels.
slots.contents-list: What is included in the pledge or launch package.
slots.compatibility-strip: Works with, integrations, sizing, OS, or audience fit.
slots.proof-stamps: Prototype status, guarantee, press proof, or maker credibility.
slots.shipping-note: Delivery window, beta wave, manufacturing status, or digital access timing.

manual-foldout.description: Instruction-manual style foldout with numbered specs, diagrams, and usage scenarios.

slots.numbered-spec: Repeated numbered feature or spec row.
slots.diagram-slot: Product diagram, screenshot, or simplified flow.
slots.usage-scenario: Short scenario copy showing why the product matters.

visual_brief: A back-of-box product panel, like the rear of a toy or gadget package. It should explain the offer with practical detail while keeping a playful launch tone. Use structured zones, thick separators, sticker-like labels, and readable body text. Do not turn each feature into a floating card.

responsive.mobile: Stack feature bursts, contents, compatibility, and notes. Use accordions for secondary details.
responsive.tablet: Two-column manual layout with specs and proof beside a diagram.
responsive.desktop: Asymmetric package-back layout with a large contents/spec column and smaller proof/shipping panels.

layout_hints.content_order: Explain what it is, what is included, who it is for, and when/how backers receive it.
layout_hints.trust: Campaign caveats should be visible and plain, not hidden in tiny legal copy.
layout_hints.visual_language: Use package/manual framing rather than SaaS feature grids.

micro.stamp-hover: proof stamp tilts or brightens slightly
micro.accordion-open: manual panel expands instantly with no bouncy motion

keyboard: Accordion sections must use button semantics and expose expanded state.
Proof stamps should have text labels, not only icons.
